question_text: A spring is stretched by 10 cm when a 2 N force is applied. The same spring is then compressed by 5 cm. Which statement correctly compares the spring forces?

options.label: A
options.text: The compression force is 1 N because compression only produces half the force of extension

options.label: B
options.text: The compression force is 1 N because the displacement is half, and force is proportional to displacement

options.label: C
options.text: The compression force is 2 N because springs always produce equal forces in both directions

options.label: D
options.text: The compression force is 4 N because compression is stronger than extension

explanation: Using Hooke's Law (F = kx), the spring constant k = 2 N ÷ 0.1 m = 20 N/m. When compressed by 5 cm (0.05 m), the force is 20 × 0.05 = 1 N. Springs obey the same law for both extension and compression.
